campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council discussed and voted on the new ordinance.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance department presented the annual update to tax rates
City council debated and made a decision on the new rates
Changes take effect in the next fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first step of reviewing the ordinance and instead scheduled it to be voted on and approved at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance being fast-tracked for approval
Council waived standard first reading process
Final vote scheduled for next regular meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting records from the previous session and reviewed the town's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign off on several construction contracts and professional service agreements to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed town finances and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction services
Approved town improvement projects and three-year professional service agreements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. The council reviewed and finalized the agenda for their May 5 meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and looked ahead to plan the May 12 meeting agenda.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 meeting agenda and added any last-minute items
Set time slots for specific agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026
Began planning the May 12, 2026 draft agenda

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review a draft plan for improving biodiversity and urban forests in the city, then held a regular session to recognize the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center for an award and declare April as Fair Housing Month.
keyTopics: Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan - draft review and feedback
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center receives California Parks and Recreation Society Award of Excellence
Fair Housing Month Proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comments.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 27, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each. The council also approved the minutes from a previous January audit meeting and reviewed how well the city's retirement and health benefit trust funds performed during the first three months of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any topic
Approval of January 26 audit committee meeting minutes
Retirement and health benefit fund performance report for Q1 2026
State law limits council decisions to items officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The city council met to discuss allowing large graphic signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union for 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighters contract agreement approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to address several financial and development matters. Key items included approving tax policies, setting the budget for the next fiscal year (2026-27), updating user fees, and discussing efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees
Updates to city user fees and charges
County homelessness prevention initiatives
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several park and recreation projects, including improvements to the Foothills Nature Preserve and the future of the city's middle school athletics program. The council also reviewed the Parks and Recreation Commission's work plan for the coming year and received updates from various committees and city departments.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve improvements project
Middle school athletics program management and direction
Parks and Recreation Commission work plan for 2027
Ad hoc committee and liaison updates
